name: Utilitarian
summary: A work-focused style that favors dense clarity, predictable controls, restrained color, and efficient scanning.

palette: neutral backgrounds, clear text, functional status colors
surfaces: flat panels, tables, toolbars, and form regions
shapes: small-radius rectangles and stable control frames
layout: dense but organized grids, sidebars, and tables
imagery: functional screenshots, diagrams, and data previews

avoid: Avoid decorative marketing tone in task surfaces.

recommendations: Use neutral surfaces and semantic status colors.
Use compact readable type with tabular clarity.
avoid: Avoid oversized hero type inside tools.

recommendations: Use clear command hierarchy and predictable sizes.
avoid: Avoid ambiguous icon-only controls without labels/tooltips.

recommendations: Use panels only when grouping helps scanning.
avoid: Avoid card-heavy dashboards when tables fit better.

recommendations: Use stable app navigation and search/filter access.
avoid: Avoid marketing-style nav in app surfaces.

recommendations: Use minimal footer or utility links.
Use sortable columns, filters, and clear density.
avoid: Avoid hiding primary data in cards.

recommendations: Use explicit labels, validation, and recovery.
Use clear row, button, and menu affordances.
Use immediate panel and menu behavior.
Use clear layering for drawers, dialogs, and popovers.
Use inline validation, status, and undo where useful.

accessibilityRisks.risk: Dense UI can reduce touch target size and reading comfort.
accessibilityRisks.mitigation: Define minimum control sizes, focus states, and responsive density rules.

css: Use stable dimensions, compact spacing, visible focus, and semantic status colors.
avoid: Do not make operational tools look like landing pages.
